SEARCH
Module DSA
Package com.dsa.pattern

Class BitmaskDP

java.lang.Object
com.dsa.pattern.BitmaskDP
public class BitmaskDP extends Object
Demonstrates the Bitmask Dynamic Programming (Bitmask DP) Pattern.

Bitmask DP is used for problems involving subsets of elements where the number of elements is small (usually ≤ 20), and the state can be represented efficiently as a bitmask integer. Common applications include Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP), subset sum variants, and combinatorial optimization.

Key Idea

  • Use an integer to represent subsets of elements, where each bit indicates inclusion/exclusion.
  • DP state: dp[mask] = optimal value for subset represented by mask.
  • Transition: iterate over all elements not yet included and update dp[mask | (1 invalid input: '<'invalid input: '<' i)].
  • Reduces memory and runtime compared to storing subsets as arrays or lists.

When to Use

  • Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) for ≤ 20 cities
  • Subset sum / combinatorial optimization with small N
  • Problems involving all subsets of a set where N is small

Example Implementation (TSP)


 package com.dsa.pattern;

 public class BitmaskDPExample {

 	// Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) using Bitmask DP
 	static int tsp(int[][] dist) {
 		int n = dist.length;
 		int[][] dp = new int[1 << n][n];
 		for (int i = 0; i < (1 << n); i++)
 			for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
 				dp[i][j] = Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2; // avoid overflow

 		dp[1][0] = 0; // start at node 0, mask 1<<0

 		for (int mask = 1; mask < (1 << n); mask++) {
 			for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) {
 				if ((mask & (1 << u)) == 0)
 					continue;
 				for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
 					if ((mask & (1 << v)) != 0)
 						continue;
 					dp[mask | (1 << v)][v] = Math.min(dp[mask | (1 << v)][v], dp[mask][u] + dist[u][v]);
 				}
 			}
 		}

 		int ans = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
 		for (int u = 1; u < n; u++)
 			ans = Math.min(ans, dp[(1 << n) - 1][u] + dist[u][0]);
 		return ans;
 	}

 	public static void main(String[] args) {
 		int[][] dist = { { 0, 10, 15, 20 }, { 10, 0, 35, 25 }, { 15, 35, 0, 30 }, { 20, 25, 30, 0 } };
 		System.out.println("Minimum TSP tour cost: " + tsp(dist));
 	}
 }

Complexity

  • Time: O(2^N * N^2) for TSP with N nodes
  • Space: O(2^N * N) for DP table

Applications

  • Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP)
  • Subset sum / subset optimization for small N
  • Bitmask combinatorial problems
